data.title: Global economic freedom has decreased for the fourth consecutive year according to the Fraser Institute report.
data.description: According to the latest Economic Freedom of the World report released by the Fraser Institute today in Toronto on September 25, 2025, global economic freedom has decreased for the fourth consecutive year. The report highlights that after reaching its peak in 2019, economic freedom has been on a downward trend, with this year marking the continuous decline. This concerning trend underscores a notable shift in policy dynamics, affecting various economies worldwide.

data.title: USA Buyer Program for IGJS Jaipur Show 2026 appoints Olga Gonzalez as USA Coordinator, offering stipends, factory tours, and support for U.S. participants.
data.description: USA Coordinator Olga Gonzalez is providing U.S. retailers, manufacturers, and designers with exclusive access to India's booming gem and jewelry industry. This opportunity includes stipends, factory tours, and personalized support. The initiative was launched in New York on September 25, 2025. This program offers a unique chance to tap into India's flourishing sector and create fruitful partnerships.

data.title: AXIL Brands introduces X30i Filtered Earplug Bundle Pack on Costco.com, 2nd product launch on the platform.
data.description: The X30i earplugs mark the company's second product release on the Costco platform.

data.title: Vestiaire Collective releases first menswear resale report, part of strategy to expand menswear with a dedicated category.
data.description: On September 25, 2025, Vestiaire Collective, a prominent global platform specializing in pre-owned luxury fashion, introduced their inaugural report focusing on men's circular fashion. This significant move is part of the company's initiative to broaden its menswear offerings by establishing a distinct category.

data.title: USound & Gettop sign deal for MEMS speakers, Gettop to manufacture speakers using USound's technology, guaranteed volumes.
data.description: In Graz, Austria, USound, a prominent company specializing in MEMS speakers and AI-driven acoustic sensor solutions, has recently formed a strategic partnership with Gettop, a leading global provider of acoustic components. The agreement between the two companies involves Gettop producing MEMS speakers utilizing USound's cutting-edge MEMS wafers and ASICs. The deal outlines specified production quantities and sets the groundwork for a strong alliance in sales and market expansion efforts. data.title: ESET Research uncovers North Korean group DeceptiveDevelopment's crypto theft schemes using fake job offers targeting freelance developers across platforms, employing sophisticated social engineering tactics to steal cryptocurrency and engage in possible cyberespionage. The group focuses on financial gains, especially in cryptocurrency and Web3 projects, operating since 2023. They use malware like BeaverTail and OtterCookie to exfiltrate data. Findings presented at VB Conference.
data.description: ESET Research released a report on the activities of the DeceptiveDevelopment threat group linked to North Korean IT workers, focusing on cryptocurrency theft, with an aim towards cyberespionage. The group targets freelance developers on Windows, Linux, and macOS platforms. They use social engineering tactics like fake job interviews and the ClickFix technique to distribute malware. The group has been active since at least 2023 and has evolved from basic malware to more advanced toolsets. The research also delves into the involvement of North Korean IT workers in fraudulent employment schemes connected to DeceptiveDevelopment. The findings were presented at the Virus Bulletin Conference. The group primarily uses payloads like BeaverTail, OtterCookie, and WeaselStore infostealers, along with the InvisibleFerret modular RAT. They operate similarly to Lazarus's Operation DreamJob targeting developers in cryptocurrency and Web3 projects. The report provides detailed insights into their operations and methods.

data.title: Clothing company takes on the US in legal battle over trade policy, facing challenges of domestic manufacturing. #PrincessAwesome
data.description: A legal dispute over President Trump's trade policy has put a children's clothing company in the spotlight. The company, which has faced difficulties with domestic manufacturing for years, is now at the center of this issue.

data.title: AGCO Power invests €54 million for new capacity, promoting circular economy. AGCO is a global leader in agriculture machinery.
data.description: AGCO, a leading global company in agricultural machinery and precision ag technology, recently disclosed plans to strengthen their circular economy initiative. On September 25, 2025, in Duluth, Georgia, the company revealed that its AGCO Power™ division will be expanding its production capacity. With AGCO listed on the NYSE as AGCO, this development signifies a pivotal move towards sustainability and efficiency within the industry.

data.title: Arbitrum (ARB) price dips by 5.12% to $0.42, near key support at a RSI of 34.37, hinting at oversold levels.
data.description: The price of ARB has decreased by 5.12% to $0.42, nearing crucial support levels. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is currently at 34.37, indicating possible oversold conditions for this layer-2 token.

data.title: XCMG Crane of XCMG Machinery (SHE: 000425) saw significant international growth in Q3 2025, with many premium cranes delivered.
data.description: In Xuzhou, China, as of September 25, 2025, XCMG Crane, a division of XCMG Machinery (SHE: 000425), successfully completed numerous premium crane shipments to major international markets by the conclusion of the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Price of APT drops by 4.4% to $4.12, testing critical support level. Oversold conditions may offer buying chances.
data.description: The price of APT has dropped to $4.12, reaching critical support levels. Technical indicators are signaling oversold conditions, potentially offering buying opportunities for traders willing to take on risk.

data.title: Circle is exploring 'reversible' USDC transactions to integrate stablecoins into the financial system.
data.description: The president of Circle suggests that stablecoins could become an integral component of the traditional financial system due to their transaction reversibility feature. This development highlights the potential for stablecoins to establish a crucial presence in legacy financial institutions.
